titles.text: Heracles no Eikou III - Kamigami no Chinmoku
ヘラクレスの栄光III 神々の沈黙

roms.name: Heracles no Eikou III - Kamigami no Chinmoku (Japan)

descriptions.text:
「ヘラクレスの栄光III 神々の沈黙」の第三作目では、再び古代ギリシャ神話の世界を訪れます。遠くの村から来た若い少年の役割を担い、平和な生活を送っていましたが、ある日村の豚が狂い始め、その結果地下に投げ込まれてしまいます。そこで神秘的な妖精たちに救われますが、何かがうまくいかず、あなたが知っていた世界はもはや存在しません。バランスを取り戻すために、偉大な神々やギリシャ神話の他の有名なキャラクターたちと接触を図らなければなりません。

シリーズの前作と同様に、これはターン制の戦闘を持つ標準的なコンソールスタイルのRPGで、視点は一人称です。昼夜のサイクルがあり、これが世界地図や町の外観だけでなく、住人たちのスケジュールにも影響を与えます。

Heracles no Eikou III: Kamigami no Chinmoku is the third installment of the series. Originally released for the Super Famicom, it was released on the Japanese Virtual Console in 2007 and a mobile phone in 2008 by G-Mode.

The game's plot and setting are loosely based around various episodes included in Greek mythology. The hero begins the game in a state of total memory-loss, but discovers that he has somehow acquired a mysterious power that shields him from all bodily harm, essentially making him immortal. The only clue the hero has in discovering his own identity is a mysterious dream that he sees every night, and he begins his journey in search of the location shown in the dream.

The gameplay borrows various motifs from ancient mythology, particularly the concept of immortality. An immortal character can execute certain actions that other characters cannot, like jumping off high cliffs without being injured. The game's writer, Kazushige Nojima, composed a storyline where the hero encounters the Greek gods while trying to discover his own true identity. Like the game's predecessor, Heracles no Eikō II: Titan no Metsubō, the game focuses on its rich storyline rather than the game system, which does not differ significantly from other role-playing video games of the period.
